finding_text: Low condenser-water flow alarm LF-203. Pump running at 47 Hz. Operator noted discharge pressure lower than normal.
followup_text: Checked suction valve handle open. No strainer DP taps installed. Open reliability notification for flow and vibration review.

finding_text: CT-2 basin screen had algae mat and leaves. Fan running. Approach high during afternoon load.
followup_text: Screen rinsed. No pump work performed.

finding_text: Basket pulled. Moderate biofilm and pea gravel. No calibrated DP because upstream and downstream taps are not yet installed.
followup_text: Basket cleaned. Recommend temporary DP taps before next diagnosis.

finding_text: Elastomer spider had orange dust and one compressed lobe. Replaced spider. No missing lugs. Alignment not checked with laser.
followup_text: Vibration lower during brief run but remained above alert. Open alignment work order.

finding_text: BAS screen shows CT-2 fan DE bearing at 86 deg C. Route tech confirmed sensor is on CWP-3 drive-end bearing housing.
followup_text: Control shop to correct BAS label. Use route point for component identity until display is corrected.

finding_text: Seal gland wet and intermittent drip to drain pan. Flush panel had no local pressure gauge installed. HMI flow looked low.
followup_text: Wiped seal area. Plan seal inspection and install local flush gauge.

finding_text: Installed temporary upstream and downstream strainer pressure taps. First stable DP check deferred until pump run at normal speed.
followup_text: Technician marked taps installed at 14:30 in field note; CMMS start time only reflects job release.

finding_text: Close-out text states inboard mechanical seal replaced and leak stopped. Completion tab lacks technician initials.
followup_text: Permit close time 12:10 is earlier than close-out. Parts issue not attached. Post-work operator note still reports mist at guard drain.

finding_text: Planner requested correct seal kit for WO-78151. Store room response: reservation only, no issue; job cancelled as duplicate after production restart.
followup_text: Does not validate seal installation under WO-78151.

finding_text: Contractor marked alignment PASS using general site tolerance sheet. Horizontal offset and angular readings remain outside OEM tolerance.
followup_text: Suction pipe support clamp tightened after final reading. No repeat laser measurement captured.

finding_text: Local flush pressure gauge installed. First reading 19 psi while pump was running at 50 Hz and seal mist present.
followup_text: Minimum flush pressure requires comparison to OEM criteria. Flush filter not changed under this order.

finding_text: CT-2 basin screen 60 percent matted. Fan gearbox oil at lower sight glass mark. Approach was high before cleaning.
followup_text: Screen cleaned and gearbox topped off. Approach improved later in day; pump vibration and seal leak persisted.

finding_text: Clamp-on ultrasonic meter read 1325 gpm while BAS showed 995 gpm at same speed. Loop card scale and BAS scale mismatch suspected.
followup_text: Controls to perform loop scaling correction. Do not use pre-correction BAS flow alone for pump curve comparison.

finding_text: GB-CT2 oil level midpoint after top-off. Gearbox vibration normal. No evidence tying tower gearbox to CWP-3 DE bearing temperature.
followup_text: Continue routine monitoring.

finding_text: BAS scaling corrected from 0-2000 gpm to 0-1500 gpm to match loop card. Post-correction BAS flow matched ultrasonic within 2.5 percent.
followup_text: Use post-correction BAS values as measured flow. Pre-correction values require rescale.

finding_text: Basket 70 percent blinded with tower fill chips, biofilm, and gasket fragments. Pre-clean DP 8.4 psi at 50 Hz. Post-clean DP 2.1 psi.
followup_text: Flow improved but vibration remained in alarm range. Investigate tower debris source and pump train alignment.

finding_text: BAS command 18 percent and feedback 21 percent during high approach event. No mechanical stroke calibration record attached.
followup_text: Valve trend does not explain pump vibration or seal leak. Use as tower temperature contributor only.

finding_text: DE bearing temperature reached 91 deg C on 2026-05-12. No bearing defect frequencies in route notes. Bearing not inspected because alignment remains unresolved.
followup_text: Inspect bearing only after alignment corrected or if temperature remains above stop threshold.

finding_text: Low-flow alarm during Chiller 1 loading. BAS flow now scaled correctly; flow lower than curve expectation at 47 Hz. Strainer DP 3.6 psi, not action level.
followup_text: Remaining low flow appears pump performance/alignment related, not strainer-only.

finding_text: Flush filter element replaced. Pressure increased from 19 psi to 27 psi at 50 Hz. Seal still showed wet ring at gland after 30 minute run.
followup_text: Flush restriction improved, but seal condition not fully resolved. Correct seal replacement remains unverified.

finding_text: Chiller 1 trip followed low condenser-water flow alarm. CWP-3 at 47 Hz. Tower approach normal at trip time. Strainer DP 3.4 psi.
followup_text: Trip impact tied to pump flow shortfall. No independent chiller fault found.

finding_text: Reliability review requested for unresolved alignment, seal, and pump performance conflicts.
followup_text: Do not close until inventory, alignment, route, pump curve, and flush evidence reconciled.

finding_text: Motor current balanced. No evidence of phase imbalance. Motor vibration lower than pump DE readings.
followup_text: Electrical motor fault not supported by evidence.

finding_text: Planner used wrong asset CWP-3A in replacement seal planning line. No inspection performed.
followup_text: Correct asset is CWP-3. Do not create separate CWP-3A asset.
